title: 10 best herbal capsules for stomach pain

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger capsules helps with stomach pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract. The capsule's active compound, gingerol, works to calm down the stomach muscles and ease cramps, nausea, and bloating. Additionally, ginger has been shown to improve digestion by increasing gastric motility, reducing spasms, and alleviating symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS). As a result, ginger capsules provide natural relief from stomach pain and discomfort, making them an effective alternative to over-the-counter medications.
remedy_properties: Shogaol: Shogaol is a bioactive compound found in ginger that has potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, helping to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain in the stomach and digestive tract.

Gingerols: Gingerols are another type of bioactive compound found in ginger that have natural antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to reduce stomach pain and discomfort by inhibiting the growth of harmful bacteria and reducing inflammation.

Zingibain: Zingibain is a proteolytic enzyme found in ginger that has been shown to have antispasmodic properties, helping to relax the muscles in the stomach and intestines and reduce spasms that can contribute to stomach pain.
remedy_constituents: Gingerol: Gingerol is a type of phenolic compound that helps reduce inflammation and relax the muscles in the digestive tract, providing relief from stomach pain and nausea.
Shogaol: Shogaol is a bioactive compound that has anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce inflammation in the stomach and alleviate pain caused by stomach ulcers or acid reflux.

6-Gingerol: 6-Gingerol is a phenolic compound that exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to reduce inflammation and protect the stomach lining from damage, thereby alleviating stomach pain.
remedy_parts: Buds: Buds: the immature, swelling parts of the plant that will eventually grow into new flowers or leaves, used for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1-2 pounds of dried zingiber officinale root and sift it through a fine mesh to remove any debris.
Dry the sifted root in a low-temperature oven at 150°f for 2 hours to remove any moisture.
Grind the dried root into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder.
Mix 300-500 mg of the ground root powder with 10-20% of a binding agent such as gelatin or cellulose.
Fill 0.5-1 ml gelatin capsules with the mixture and seal them properly to preserve the herbal powder.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kava capsules helps with stomach pain because they contain the active compounds, kavalactones, which have a natural anti-inflammatory and anxiolytic effect. These compounds help to soothe the digestive system, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with stomach pain. By relaxing the muscles in the stomach and intestines, kava capsules can alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), acid reflux, and other gastrointestinal disorders, providing fast and effective relief from stomach ache.
remedy_properties: Kavalactones: Kavalactones are a group of compounds found in kava root that have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, helping to reduce stomach pain and discomfort by blocking the production of inflammatory chemicals.

Antioxidant activity: Kava's antioxidant properties help protect the stomach lining from damage caused by free radicals, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress that can contribute to stomach pain.
Muscle relaxant effects: Kavalactones in kava capsules also have a muscle relaxant effect on the digestive tract, helping to reduce spasms and cramping that can exacerbate stomach pain.
remedy_constituents: Kavalactones: These compounds help with stomach pain by reducing inflammation and modulating the gut's nervous system, thereby decreasing discomfort and pain.
Alkaloids: Alkaloids in kava have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to soothe stomach irritation and reduce pain associated with digestive issues.
Steroids: The steroids present in kava capsules have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation in the stomach and alleviate pain caused by digestive disorders.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: They contain a high concentration of kavalactones, which are responsible for the plant's medicinal properties and provide relief from stomach pain.
Roots: The roots of Piper methysticum have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including stomach problems, due to their high content of kavalactones.
Leaves: The leaves of the plant also contain kavalactones, which are believed to help alleviate stomach pain and other digestive issues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh roots of piper methysticum when they are fully grown and free from signs of decay.
Dry the harvested roots in a warm and well-ventilated area for 7 to 10 days to reduce moisture content to 10%.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or a coffee grinder.
Mix the powdered roots with a binding agent such as vegetable cellulose or gelatin in a 1:2 ratio by weight to form a uniform blend.
Encapsulate the powdered blend in gelatin or vegetable cellulose capsules using a manual or automatic capsule filling machine.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cumin capsules helps with stomach pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe digestive issues such as bloating, cramps, and discomfort. The antioxidants present in cumin also help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS). Additionally, cumin's carminative effects can ease gas and indigestion, promoting a healthy gut environment and reducing stomach pain and discomfort. By taking herbal cumin capsules, individuals can experience relief from persistent stomach issues and enjoy improved digestive health.
remedy_properties: Carminative Property: Cumin's carminative property helps to relieve stomach pain by reducing gas and bloating, which can exacerbate discomfort.
Antioxidant Property: Cumin's antioxidant property helps to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to stomach pain and discomfort.
Digestive Enzyme Stimulant Property: Cumin's ability to stimulate digestive enzymes can help alleviate stomach pain by improving digestion and reducing symptoms of indigestion and bloating.
remedy_constituents: Cuminaldehyde: This compound acts as an anti-inflammatory agent, which helps reduce inflammation in the stomach and alleviate pain.
Cumic acid: Cumic acid has antioxidant properties that help protect the stomach lining from damage and reduce oxidative stress, leading to a decrease in stomach pain and discomfort.
Limonene: Limonene, a terpene present in cumin, exhibits carminative properties, which means it helps relieve gas and bloating in the digestive system, thereby reducing stomach pain and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Seeds: They are commonly used due to their high concentration of carminative properties, which help alleviate stomach discomfort and pain.
Leaves: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help soothe and calm the stomach.
Stems: They are used due to their ability to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing stomach pain and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of cuminum cyminum seeds from mature plants that are free of contaminants and pests.
Clean the seeds by rinsing them with distilled water to remove dirt and debris.
Dry the seeds in a single layer at 35 degrees celsius for 24 hours to remove excess moisture.
Grind the dried seeds into a fine powder using a mill or grinder to increase bioavailability.
Fill gelatin capsules with 250 milligrams of the powdered cuminum cyminum and seal them properly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Coriander capsules helps with stomach pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively reduce inflammation in the digestive tract. The herb also possesses carminative properties, helping to alleviate gas and bloating that can contribute to abdominal discomfort. Additionally, coriander's antispasmodic effects help relax the muscles in the stomach, reducing cramping and spasms associated with stomach pain. This natural remedy provides a gentle and effective way to soothe digestive issues, promoting overall gut health and well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Coriander capsules contain compounds that reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which helps alleviate stomach pain and discomfort caused by inflammation.

Carminative properties: The active ingredients in coriander capsules can help expel gas from the digestive system, reducing bloating and cramping associated with indigestion and irritable bowel syndrome (IBS).
Antioxidant properties: Coriander's antioxidant properties help protect the stomach lining from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate stomach pain and inflammation.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: A terpene that acts as an anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic, helping to reduce muscle spasms in the stomach and alleviate pain.
Linalyl acetate: A terpene that has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, helping to reduce pain and inflammation in the stomach and alleviate digestive discomfort.

Kaempferol: A flavonoid that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to protect the stomach lining from damage, reduce inflammation, and alleviate pain associated with stomach ulcers and gastritis.
remedy_parts: Seeds: The seeds of Coriandrum sativum are commonly used to make capsules for stomach pain due to their carminative and anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: The leaves of Coriandrum sativum are used in capsule form to alleviate stomach pain due to their ability to reduce inflammation and relieve digestive issues.
Seeds: The seeds are again used in capsule form due to their high concentration of essential oils, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe stomach pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of coriandrum sativum leaves and stems when they reach optimal maturity after 70 to 90 days.
Dry the harvested material in a well-ventilated area for 2 to 3 weeks at 35 degrees celsius.
Grind 20 grams of dried coriandrum sativum into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix the powder with 10 grams of gelatin in a mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 0.5 to 1 gram of the coriandrum sativum powder mixture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric capsules helps with stomach pain because of its potent anti-inflammatory compound, curcumin. When curcumin is ingested in capsule form, it targets inflammation in the digestive tract, reducing swelling and discomfort caused by conditions like irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), gastritis, and ulcerative colitis. By alleviating these underlying causes, turmeric capsules can provide rapid relief from stomach pain, cramping, and bloating, promoting a sense of calm and overall gut well-being.
remedy_properties: Curcumin: A powerful anti-inflammatory compound that reduces inflammation and alleviates stomach pain by blocking the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.
Cineole: A natural antispasmodic agent that helps relax smooth muscle contractions in the digestive tract, reducing cramps, spasms, and pain associated with stomach issues.
Volatile oils: Which have antimicrobial properties that help combat Helicobacter pylori (H. pylori) bacteria, a common cause of stomach ulcers and gastritis, thereby reducing inflammation and pain.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: Curcumin helps with stomach pain by reducing inflammation and inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, thus providing relief from pain and discomfort.
Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): DMC helps with stomach pain by exerting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which help to protect the stomach lining and alleviate pain and inflammation.

Bisdemethoxycurcumin (BDMC): BDMC helps with stomach pain by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and enzymes, which are involved in the development of stomach pain and inflammation.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Curcuma longa Rhyzomes: The rhizomes of Curcuma longa are used to make capsules for stomach pain because they contain curcumin, a compound with anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties.
Rhyzomes: Curcuma longa Rhyzomes: They are also used due to their ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with stomach pain and other digestive issues.
Rhyzomes: Curcuma longa Rhyzomes: Additionally, the rhizomes are used for their antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, which can help prevent infections and promote overall gut health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of dried curcuma longa root with 2% curcumin content and store in an airtight container.
Grind the dried root into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix 1 gram of the ground powder with 1 gram of cellulose powder and 1 gram of magnesium stearate.
Fill the mixture into empty gelatin capsules using a capsule filling machine for 10 minutes.
Label and store the capsules in a cool dry place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea capsules helps with stomach pain because they contain potent herbs that soothe the digestive system, calming inflammation and reducing discomfort. The capsules allow for a precise delivery of these herbal compounds to the affected area, promoting relaxation and ease in the stomach and abdomen. Additionally, many herbal teas have natural anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with stomach issues such as bloating, cramps, and digestive disorders.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Many herbal teas, such as ginger and turmeric, contain anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which may alleviate symptoms of stomach pain.

Antispasmodic properties: Herbs like peppermint and chamomile have antispasmodic properties that can help relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramping that can cause stomach pain.

Soothing astringent compounds: Herbal teas like marshmallow root and licorice root contain soothing astringent compounds that can help calm irritation and inflammation in the digestive tract, providing relief from stomach pain.
remedy_constituents: Catechins: Catechins, particularly epigallocatechin gallate (EGCG), help alleviate stomach pain by reducing inflammation and inhibiting the production of prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that cause pain and inflammation in the stomach.
Quercetin: Quercetin is a flavonoid that has anti-inflammatory properties, which help soothe and reduce inflammation in the stomach, thereby providing relief from stomach pain.

Theaflavins: Theaflavins are a class of flavonoids found in green tea, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help protect the stomach lining from damage, reduce inflammation, and alleviate stomach pain.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Camellia sinensis are commonly used to make capsules for stomach pain because they contain compounds like theaflavins and thearubigins, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help soothe stomach issues.

Buds: Camellia sinensis buds are often used to make capsules for stomach pain because they are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which can help reduce inflammation and alleviate stomach discomfort.

(Optional) Stems: Camellia sinensis stems are sometimes used to make capsules for stomach pain because they contain compounds like catechins and theaflavins, which can help reduce inflammation and promote digestive health. However, this is less commonly used compared to leaves and buds.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried camellia sinensis leaves from a reputable source and ensure quality control.
Clean the leaves by gently blowing off any debris and then air-dry them for 24 hours.
Grind 50g of the dried leaves into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix the powder with 50g of gelatin and 50g of glycerin to create a uniform mixture.
Fill empty capsules with the mixture using a capsule filler and seal them immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chinese rhubarb capsules helps with stomach pain because they contain a unique blend of natural herbs that work together to soothe and calm the digestive system. The capsules help to reduce inflammation and irritation in the gut, which can cause stomach pain, bloating, and discomfort. Additionally, the herbal formula helps to regulate bowel movements, reducing the risk of constipation or diarrhea that can exacerbate stomach pain. remedy_properties: Astringent properties: Rhubarb's astringent properties help to reduce inflammation and congestion in the digestive tract, relieving stomach pain caused by excess mucus or bleeding.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Rhubarb's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as anthraquinones, help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating stomach pain caused by conditions like gastritis or ulcers.
remedy_constituents: Emodin: Emodin is a anthraquinone glycoside that helps with stomach pain by inhibiting the growth of bacteria and reducing inflammation in the digestive tract.
Rhein: Rhein is an anthraquinone glycoside that helps with stomach pain by stimulating bowel movements, relieving constipation, and reducing spasms in the intestinal muscles.

Alkaloids (specifically, rhemine and palmatine): The alkaloids in Rheum palmatum capsules help with stomach pain by reducing inflammation, relieving spasms, and modulating the gut microbiome to promote a healthy digestive system.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Rheum palmatum are used to treat stomach pain due to their ability to reduce inflammation and relieve digestive issues.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of Rheum palmatum are used to treat stomach pain due to their anti-inflammatory properties and ability to soothe the digestive system.
Leaves: The leaves of Rheum palmatum are used to treat stomach pain due to their diuretic properties and ability to reduce fluid retention and alleviate digestive discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-60g of fresh rheum palmatum rhizome and roots on a dry sunny day.
Dry the harvested material in a well-ventilated area for 7-10 days at 50-60°c temperature.
Grind the dried material into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 30 minutes.
Mix 5g of the powder with 5g of vegetable cellulose using a small mixing bowl for 5 minutes.
Encapsulate the mixture into 100-200 capsules using a capsule filling machine for 10 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice capsules helps with stomach pain because they contain a compound called glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and protect the mucous membranes lining the stomach. This can provide relief from conditions such as heartburn, acid reflux, and inflammatory bowel disease (IBD). Flavonoids: Licorice capsules contain flavonoids, such as glabridin and isoliquiritigenin, which have been shown to protect the mucous lining of the stomach from damage caused by stress, acid, and other factors, reducing the risk of stomach ulcers and pain.

Saponins: The saponin content in licorice root can help soothe the digestive tract by reducing spasms and inflammation, making it an effective natural remedy for relieving stomach cramps, bloating, and discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: Glycyrrhizin, a triterpenoid saponin, helps to reduce inflammation and soothe the stomach lining, thereby alleviating stomach pain and discomfort.

Flavonoids (specifically, licochalcone A): Flavonoids, particularly licochalcone A, exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help protect the stomach lining from damage, reduce inflammation, and ease stomach pain.

Isoliquiritigenin: Isoliquiritigenin, a flavonoid with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate stomach pain by modulating the immune response and protecting the stomach lining from oxidative stress.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Glycyrrhiza glabra are commonly used to make capsules for stomach pain due to their high content of glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.
Leaves: The leaves of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used to make capsules for stomach pain due to their ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of indigestion and nausea.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots from a reputable supplier.
Dry the roots in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 1-2 grams of the powder with 2-3 grams of cellulose or gelatin powder as filler.
Fill 0-00 capsules with the mixture and seal them immediately to preserve potency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile capsules helps with stomach pain because its natural anti-inflammatory properties help soothe the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort. The gentle, calming effects of chamomile also ease muscle spasms in the abdominal area, providing quick relief from cramps, bloating, and irritability. remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Chamomile contains flavonoids and terpenoids that have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate stomach pain.

remedy_constituents: Apolypodol: This sesquiterpene lactone has anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce inflammation and ease stomach pain.
Apigenin: A flavonoid that has a soothing effect on the stomach, reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation, thus alleviating stomach pain and discomfort.
Matricine: A sesquiterpene lactone with anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, helping to calm stomach cramps and ease digestive discomfort.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers of Matricaria chamomilla are commonly used due to their high content of apigenin and bisabolol, which have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that help alleviate stomach pain and inflammation.
Seeds: The seeds of Matricaria chamomilla contain essential oils, including bisabolol, which has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that help reduce stomach pain and cramps.
Leaves: The leaves of Matricaria chamomilla are used for their calming and soothing effects, which can help alleviate stomach upset and indigestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250g of matricaria chamomilla flowers when the buds are in full bloom for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested flowers in a single layer at 50°c for 2 hours to prevent oil loss.
Grind the dried flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or spice grinder.
Mix 250g of the powder with 250g of gelatin or vegetarian capsule fillers in a bowl.
Fill 100 empty capsules with the mixture using a capsule filler and seal them tightly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint capsules helps with stomach pain because they contain menthol, a natural compound that relaxes the muscles in the digestive tract. When taken orally, peppermint oil softens the stomach lining and reduces inflammation, alleviating symptoms such as cramping, bloating, and discomfort. Additionally, peppermint's cooling properties help to slow down gastric emptying, allowing the stomach to digest food more efficiently and reducing feelings of nausea and vomiting associated with stomach pain.
remedy_properties: Menthol content: Peppermint capsules contain menthol, which helps to relax the muscles in the digestive tract and reduce inflammation, providing relief from stomach cramps and spasms.

Antispasmodic effect: The natural compounds present in peppermint, such as flavonoids and terpenes, have an antispasmodic effect that can help to ease muscle contractions and spasms in the digestive tract, reducing pain and discomfort.

Digestive enzyme stimulant: Peppermint capsules also stimulate the production of digestive enzymes, which helps to improve digestion, reduce bloating, and alleviate symptoms of stomach pain and discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: Menthol has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce stomach pain and discomfort by numbing the nerves and reducing inflammation in the digestive tract.
Menthone: Menthone is known for its antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to relax the muscles in the stomach and reduce cramping and discomfort associated with stomach pain.
Limonene: Limonene has gastroprotective properties, which help to shield the stomach lining from acid damage and reduce inflammation, thereby providing relief from stomach pain and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used to make capsules for stomach pain due to their high concentration of menthol, a natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory compound.
Roots: They are used to make capsules for stomach pain due to their rich content of menthone, a compound with digestive and anti-inflammatory properties.
Rhyzomes: They are used to make capsules for stomach pain due to their high menthol and menthone content, which helps to soothe stomach discomfort and reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30g of dried menthax piperita leaves and flowers from a clean and well-maintained garden area.
Dry the harvested menthax piperita leaves and flowers in a single layer for 7 days at 40°c.
Crush 10g of dried menthax piperita leaves and flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 1g of the powdered menthax piperita with 0.5g of gelatin and 0.5g of glycerin in a small bowl.
Fill 10 empty capsules with 0.5g of the menthax piperita mixture each and seal them immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel capsules helps with stomach pain because they contain the essential oils and compounds found in the herb, which have natural anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. These properties help to soothe the digestive tract and relax the muscles that can cause cramps and spasms, reducing the severity of stomach pain. Additionally, fennel's carminative properties help to eliminate gas and bloating, further alleviating discomfort in the abdominal region.
remedy_properties: Carminative properties: Fennel's carminative properties help to expel gas and reduce bloating, which can alleviate stomach discomfort and cramps.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Fennel's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, can help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), acid reflux, and other stomach pain conditions.

Antispasmodic properties: Fennel's antispasmodic compounds, such as volatile oils, can help to relax the muscles in the digestive tract and reduce spasms that can cause stomach cramps, pain, and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots are rich in fiber and antioxidants, which help soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation in the gut.
Seeds: Fennel seeds contain anethole, a natural compound that has anti-inflammatory and carminative properties, making them effective in relieving stomach pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality foeniculum vulgare seeds in their optimal ripeness for superior potency.
Dry the harvested seeds thoroughly in a well-ventilated area or using desiccant for 2 weeks.
Grind dried foeniculum vulgare seeds into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 500mg of powdered foeniculum vulgare with 500mg of filler material like silica gel or potato starch in a bowl.
Fill empty capsules shells with the herbal mixture and seal them tightly for storage and use as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion capsules helps with stomach pain because of its unique combination of anti-inflammatory and antacid properties. The herb's active compounds, such as taraxasterol and sesquiterpenes, have been shown to soothe the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and irritation that can cause stomach discomfort. Additionally, dandelion's natural diuretic properties help to alleviate water retention, which can contribute to bloating and cramps. By promoting digestion and relaxation in the stomach lining, herbal dandelion capsules can provide fast and effective relief from stomach pain.
remedy_properties: Choleretic property: Dandelion capsules have choleretic effects, which means they stimulate the production of bile in the liver, helping to relieve gallbladder and digestive issues, including stomach pain.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in dandelion capsules help protect the stomach lining from damage caused by free radicals, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress that can contribute to stomach pain.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion capsules contain anti-inflammatory compounds like taraxasterol, which help reduce swelling and inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating stomach pain and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Dandelion roots have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with stomach pain.
Barks: The barks of the dandelion plant contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help alleviate digestive issues and soothe stomach pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500 grams of taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers from a pesticide-free area on a sunny day.
Dry the harvested plant material in a well-ventilated room or using a food dehydrator at 60°c for 24 hours.
Grind the dried plant material into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle to obtain 250 grams of taraxacum officinale extract.
Mix 50 grams of the taraxacum officinale extract with 100 grams of gelatin and 150 grams of water in a bowl, stirring until a uniform paste forms.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 500 milligrams of the taraxacum officinale mixture and seal them immediately to preserve potency for up to six months at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Milk thistle capsules helps with stomach pain because it contains silymarin, a flavonoid compound that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. When taken as an herbal supplement, milk thistle capsules can help soothe and protect the stomach lining, reducing inflammation and irritation caused by digestive disorders such as gastritis or ulcers. Additionally, milk thistle's antioxidants may also help to neutralize free radicals in the body that contribute to oxidative stress, further alleviating stomach discomfort and promoting overall digestive health.
remedy_properties: Silymarin: A flavonoid complex that protects the liver from damage, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress that can contribute to stomach pain.
Flavolignans: Compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of stomach pain such as bloating, cramps, and discomfort.
Alkylphenols: Substances that have been shown to reduce inflammation in the gut and prevent damage to the mucous lining of the stomach, helping to soothe and heal stomach pain.
remedy_parts: Seeds: The seeds are a rich source of silymarin, a flavonoid that has potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which helps to soothe and protect the digestive tract against damage.

Roots: The roots contain a high concentration of alkaloids, particularly silychristin, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, making it effective in reducing stomach pain and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality silybum marianum seeds or dried fruiting bodies from trusted sources.
Clean the harvested material thoroughly removing any debris or impurities.
Dry the cleaned material in a warm well-ventilated area for at least 24 hours.
Grind 1 part silybum marianum material with 2 parts silica gel into a fine powder using mortar and pestle.
Fill empty capsules with the powdered mixture according to label instructions.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow capsules helps with stomach pain because of its soothing properties that calm inflammation in the digestive tract. The mucilages present in marshmallows create a protective barrier that shields the stomach lining from acid and other irritants, reducing discomfort and pain associated with conditions such as heartburn, indigestion, and ulcers. Additionally, marshmallow capsules have anti-inflammatory effects that help to reduce swelling and inflammation in the digestive tract, providing relief from cramps, bloating, and spasms.
remedy_properties: Mucilage: Marshmallow mucilage forms a soothing film on the mucous membranes, protecting them from irritation and inflammation caused by acid reflux, ulcers, or other gastrointestinal issues.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Herbal marshmallow extracts contain anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and saponins, which help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate pain associated with conditions like irritable bowel syndrome (IBS).

Glycyrrhizin: Marshmallow root contains glycyrrhizin, a compound that has been shown to have anti-ulcer properties, helping to protect the stomach lining from damage caused by acid reflux or other factors.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the marshmallow plant are rich in mucilage, a thick, protective substance that coats and soothes the digestive tract, providing relief from stomach pain and inflammation.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the marshmallow plant contain saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce stomach discomfort and ease digestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest althaea officinalis leaves and flowers from a trusted source and ensure they are free of contaminants and pesticides.
Dry the plant material in a warm, well-ventilated area or using a food dehydrator at a temperature below 60°c for 24 hours.
Combine one part dried althaea officinalis with two parts carrier herb such as psyllium husk and store in an airtight container for future use.
Grind the combined herbal mixture into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle, then mix with 500mg of gelatin capsules to create the desired dosage.
Fill the prepared capsules within one week of grinding the herbal mixture and label with the ingredients, dosage, and preparation date for future reference.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian capsules helps with stomach pain because of its natural ability to soothe and calm the digestive system. The herb's active compounds, including valerenic acid and isovaleric acid, work to relax muscle contractions and reduce inflammation in the gastrointestinal tract. This can provide fast and effective relief from cramps, bloating, and discomfort caused by irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), indigestion, and other stomach issues. As a result, herbal valerian capsules can help alleviate symptoms of digestive discomfort and promote a sense of overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Valerian root helps to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramps that can cause stomach pain.

Carminative property: Valerian root has natural carminative properties, which means it helps to release trapped gas from the digestive system, alleviating bloating and discomfort associated with stomach pain.
Sedative property: Valerian root has a calming effect on the nervous system, reducing stress and anxiety that can exacerbate stomach pain and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the valerian plant contain a high concentration of valerenic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, making them effective in relieving stomach pain.
Rhizomes: Rhizomes are used due to their high content of sesquiterpenes, which help to calm digestive issues and reduce inflammation in the stomach.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh valeriana officinalis roots, clean, and chop them into small pieces for drying.
Dry the chopped valerian root in a dehydrator at 60°c for 24 hours or until brittle.
Grind dried valerian root into a fine powder using a spice grinder, ensuring no lumps remain.
Mix the powdered valerian root with gelatin and water to create a uniform mixture, then fill capsules.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months or longer.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hawthorn capsules helps with stomach pain because they contain bioflavonoids, flavones, and other plant compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties. These active ingredients help to soothe and calm the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and irritation that can cause stomach discomfort. Hawthorn also contains a compound called quercetin, which has been shown to block the production of enzymes that contribute to acid reflux and heartburn, providing natural relief from stomach pain and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anthocyanins: Hawthorn berries contain anthocyanins, powerful antioxidants that can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the digestive tract, which may alleviate stomach pain.

Flavonoids: The flavonoid compounds present in hawthorn, such as quercetin and kaempferol, have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the stomach, reducing pain and discomfort.

Tannins: Hawthorn contains a type of polyphenolic compound called tannins, which can help reduce inflammation and alleviate digestive issues by binding to irritants and toxins in the gut, thereby reducing their ability to cause pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the hawthorn plant contain flavonoids and terpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe stomach discomfort.
Barks: The barks of the hawthorn plant are rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant properties that aid in reducing inflammation and alleviating stomach pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500 grams of dried crataegus monogyna leaves and stems from a reputable source.
Grind the dried herb into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 30 minutes.
Mix 20% of the powdered herbal extract with 80% gelatin capsules in a ratio of 1:4 by weight.
Fill gelatin capsules with the mixture using a capsule filling machine or scoop-and-fill method, yielding 100 capsules.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to one year, protecting from moisture and light.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Beach rose capsules helps with stomach pain because of their unique ability to soothe and calm the digestive system. The herbal extract in these capsules, known for its anti-inflammatory properties, works to reduce inflammation and irritation in the stomach lining, thus alleviating symptoms such as cramps, bloating, and discomfort. Additionally, beach rose capsules have been traditionally used to ease digestive issues caused by stress, anxiety, and poor diet, making them a natural and effective remedy for relieving stomach pain.
remedy_properties: Astringent properties: Beach Rose capsules have astringent properties, which help to reduce inflammation and shrink swollen tissues in the digestive tract, providing relief from stomach pain and discomfort.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in Beach Rose capsules help to protect the stomach lining from damage caused by free radicals, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation that can contribute to stomach pain.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Beach Rose capsules contain anti-inflammatory compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help to reduce swelling and inflammation in the digestive tract, providing relief from stomach pain and cramping.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective in soothing digestive issues.
Barks: Containing phenolic acids and flavonoids, they help reduce inflammation and ease cramps associated with stomach pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rosa rugosa flowers and leaves at dawn or before noon on a sunny day.
Dry the harvested plants in a warm oven at 60°c for 24 hours or in shade for 72 hours.
Grind 30 grams of dried rosa rugosa flowers and leaves into fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground herbs with 100ml of cold-pressed vegetable oil and 10ml of glycerin in a glass container.
Fill empty capsules with the herbal mixture and seal immediately, storing in a cool dry place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe capsules helps with stomach pain because they contain the gel of the aloe plant, which has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that can help to calm irritation and inflammation in the digestive tract. The gel also contains antioxidants that can help to reduce oxidative stress and protect the gut lining from damage. By taking these capsules regularly, individuals may experience relief from stomach pain, bloating, and discomfort caused by irritable bowel syndrome, acid reflux, or other digestive issues.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Aloe vera contains anti-inflammatory compounds like aloin, aloe-emodin, and barbaloin, which help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms of stomach pain such as cramps, bloating, and discomfort.

Gel-like polysaccharides: The gel-like polysaccharides present in aloe vera capsules have been shown to soothe the mucous membranes in the gut, reducing irritation and inflammation, which can help alleviate stomach pain and discomfort.
Antioxidant activity: Aloe vera has antioxidant properties that help protect the digestive system from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to stomach pain and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Aloe roots are rich in bioactive compounds and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with stomach pain.
Leaves: Aloe leaves contain aloin, a natural laxative that helps to regulate bowel movements and soothe digestive issues, alleviating symptoms of stomach pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh aloe vera leaves from mature plants containing at least 99% gel content.
Clean the harvested leaves by rinsing them with distilled water and drying them gently.
Remove the thick green skin from the inner gel using a sterile knife or razor blade carefully.
Extract the gel contents using a centrifuge or cheesecloth within 30 minutes of harvesting to retain nutrients.
Fill the extracted aloe vera gel into gelatin capsules in proportions of 250-500mg per capsule taking 2 hours.

intro_desc: Herbal capsules for stomach pain are dietary supplements made from natural herbs and plants that help alleviate digestive discomfort and inflammation. These capsules are particularly effective in soothing stomach pain due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The best herbal capsules for stomach pain include Curcuma longa, Zingiber officinale, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Aloe barbadensis, Foeniculum vulgare, Cinchona officinalis, Mentha x piperita, Cassia angustifolia, Piper nigrum, and Ginkgo biloba. If left untreated, stomach pain can lead to decreased productivity, strained relationships, and a significant impact on overall quality of life.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to stomach pain that are treated with herbal capsules are digestive issues such as bloating, cramps, and irritable bowel syndrome (IBS). Herbal capsules containing ingredients like peppermint, ginger, and turmeric have been found to alleviate symptoms of indigestion, constipation, and diarrhea. They may also help soothe menstrual cramps and reduce inflammation in the gut.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal capsules that help with stomach pain is a blend of Peppermint, Ginger, and Licorice root. Peppermint helps to soothe and calm the digestive system, reducing inflammation and spasms. Ginger has natural anti-inflammatory properties that aid in digestion and reduce nausea. Licorice root helps to protect the stomach lining and reduce acid production, promoting a healthy gut environment. This combination can provide effective relief from stomach pain, cramps, and bloating, promoting overall digestive well-being.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Current Pharmaceutical Biotechnology, herbal capsules are beneficial for stomach pain due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The study, which extracted data from clinical and animal studies between 2004 and June 2020, found that Radix, Melissia, Glycyrrhizae, Mentha, and Liquorice were the most efficient herbal agents for visceral hypersensitivity (VH) management, particularly in irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and dyspepsia. By modulating the mRNA expression of transient receptor potential vanilloid type-1 (TRPV1) and suppressing 5-hydroxytryptamine 3 (5-HT3) or serotonin receptors, these herbal agents can effectively alleviate VH symptoms, making them a potential alternative to synthetic drugs for pain management.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa capsules helps with stomach pain because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which work to reduce inflammation and alleviate discomfort in the digestive tract. The active compound curcumin present in these capsules has been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, thereby soothing the stomach lining and promoting a sense of relief from cramps, bloating, and other stomach issues. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to provide relief from digestive discomfort, making it a popular choice for those seeking a holistic solution to stomach pain.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: It has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and pain in the stomach.
Demethoxycurcumin: It has anti-inflammatory and anti-oxidative effects that help alleviate stomach pain and discomfort caused by inflammation and oxidative stress.
Bisdemethoxycurcumin: It has anti-inflammatory and pro-apoptotic properties that help reduce inflammation and pain in the stomach by promoting the death of damaged or diseased cells.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of Curcuma longa are commonly used due to their high concentration of curcumin, a powerful anti-inflammatory compound that helps alleviate stomach pain.
Roots: The roots of Curcuma longa are utilized for their medicinal properties, including their ability to soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation in the stomach.
Leaves: The leaves of Curcuma longa are also used due to their bioactive compounds, which have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that can help alleviate stomach pain.
remedy_recipe: Source dried curcuma longa root powder from a reputable supplier in quantities of 250 grams.
Mix 250 grams of dried curcuma longa root powder with 100 grams of gelatin powder.
Use a mortar and pestle to grind the powder mixture into a fine powder for 10 minutes.
Add 50 milliliters of distilled water and 25 milliliters of glycerin to the powder mixture.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the mixture and seal them with a hand crimper.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale capsules helps with stomach pain because they contain the active compound gingerol, which has potent anti-inflammatory properties. Ginger has been traditionally used to alleviate nausea, bloating, and digestive discomfort. Its warming and soothing effects can help to calm the stomach and reduce inflammation, leading to a decrease in pain and discomfort. Furthermore, ginger's ability to stimulate digestion and improve gut motility can also help to alleviate stomach pain and promote a sense of relief.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce inflammation in the stomach and alleviate pain caused by conditions such as gastritis and ulcers.
Shogaols: Similar to gingerols, shogaols have potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help soothe and calm the stomach, providing relief from pain and discomfort.

6-Gingerol: This gingerol compound has shown to have a relaxing effect on the stomach muscles, reducing spasms and cramps that can cause pain and discomfort, making it a key constituent in alleviating stomach pain.
remedy_parts: Buds: The buds are used because they contain a higher concentration of shogaol, another active compound that has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making it effective in relieving stomach pain.
Roots: The roots are used because they contain a mix of gingerols and shogaols, making them a reliable source of the active compounds that help in alleviating stomach pain and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh zingiber officinale rhizomes in the morning when the plant is at its most potent.
Clean and dry the rhizomes for at least 7 days to remove excess moisture.
Grind the dried rhizomes into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix 500 milligrams of the powder with 100 milligrams of a natural absorbent and 100 milligrams of a natural lubricant.
Fill empty vegetarian capsules with the mixture and seal them using a capsule filling machine.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra capsules helps with stomach pain because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm the digestive tract. The licorice root extract in these capsules has been used for centuries in traditional medicine to treat gastrointestinal issues. It has been shown to reduce inflammation and irritation in the stomach and intestines, providing relief from cramps, bloating, and other discomforts associated with stomach pain. This natural remedy can also help to regulate digestive enzymes and improve overall gut health.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: Helps with stomach pain by reducing inflammation and inhibiting the production of stomach acid.
Licopyranocoumarin: Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate stomach pain and discomfort.
Isoliquiritigenin: Acts as an anti-inflammatory and antioxidant agent, which can help soothe and protect the stomach lining, reducing pain and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps reduce inflammation and soothe stomach pain.
Roots: Also contain anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that help alleviate stomach discomfort.
Roots: Are the primary source of glycyrrhetic acid, which has been shown to have gastroprotective effects and reduce stomach pain.
remedy_recipe: Dry 500g of fresh glycyrrhiza glabra roots in a dehydrator at 40°c for 24 hours.
Grind 200g of dried roots into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder.
Mix the powdered root with 100g of starch in a 1:1 ratio and 20g of silica as an excipient.
Fill gelatin capsules with 400mg of the powdered root mixture and seal them immediately.
Label and store the herbal capsules in a cool dark place to preserve their potency.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis capsules helps with stomach pain because of their soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The gel extracted from the Aloe vera plant contains compounds that calm the digestive tract and reduce inflammation, providing quick relief from stomach discomfort. The capsules also promote the healing of stomach ulcers and prevent further irritation. The natural anti-inflammatory properties of Aloe barbadensis help to alleviate cramps, bloating, and other symptoms associated with stomach pain, making it a popular natural remedy for digestive issues. Regular use can promote a healthy digestive system.
remedy_constituents: Anthraquinones: Help alleviate stomach pain by reducing inflammation and promoting bowel regularity.
Glycoproteins: Assist in soothing stomach irritation and promoting healing in the mucous membranes, thereby reducing pain and discomfort.
Polysaccharides: Help ease stomach pain by stabilizing the gut lining, reducing inflammation, and promoting the growth of beneficial gut bacteria.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high gel content, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe stomach pain and inflammation.
Leaves (specifically the inner leaf gel): Used for its ability to reduce inflammation and heal stomach ulcers.
remedy_recipe: Harvest aloe barbadensis leaves in the early morning when they are at their highest water content level.
Dry the harvested leaves in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 8 hours.
Grind the dried aloe barbadensis into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 30 seconds.
Mix 1 gram of the aloe barbadensis powder with 2 grams of gelatin in a bowl.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 500 milligrams of the aloe barbadensis gelatin mixture using a spoon.

remedies.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare capsules helps with stomach pain because they contain the active compound anethole, which has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the digestive tract. The capsules also contain essential oils that stimulate digestive enzymes, improving digestion and reducing discomfort. Additionally, Foeniculum vulgare has natural antispasmodic properties that help relax the muscles in the stomach, alleviating cramps and spasms that can cause pain. This natural remedy offers a safe and effective solution for alleviating stomach pain and promoting overall digestive health.
remedy_constituents: Foeniculin: Acts as an anti-inflammatory agent, reducing inflammation in the stomach lining and alleviating pain.
Anethole: Exhibits carminative properties, helping to expel gas from the stomach and intestines, which in turn relieves cramps and discomfort.
Apiol: Displays antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory effects, calming muscle spasms in the stomach and reducing inflammation that may be contributing to pain.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Foeniculum vulgare are used to make capsules for stomach pain because they are rich in volatile oils, particularly anethole, which has anti-inflammatory properties.

Seeds: The seeds of Foeniculum vulgare are used to make capsules for stomach pain because they contain anethole and other compounds that have carminative and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to alleviate digestive issues.

Fruits: The fruits of Foeniculum vulgare are used to make capsules for stomach pain because they contain oils and compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help soothe digestive discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried foeniculum vulgare leaves and flowers at dawn for optimal potency preservation.
Clean and dry the harvested material in a low-temperature oven at 50°c for 2 hours to remove moisture.
Grind the dried foeniculum vulgare into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 20g of the powdered foeniculum vulgare with 80g of gelatin powder to create a uniform blend.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 500mg of the foeniculum vulgare-gelatin mixture and seal them immediately.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis capsules helps with stomach pain because they contain quinine, a natural compound with anti-inflammatory properties. This helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate stomach discomfort. The capsules may also aid in regulating bowel movements and improving digestion. Additionally, Cinchona officinalis has been traditionally used to treat various gastrointestinal issues, including nausea, diarrhea, and stomach cramps. By addressing these underlying causes, Cinchona officinalis capsules may provide relief from stomach pain and promote overall digestive health. Regular use may lead to improved symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Quinine: It helps reduce inflammation and relieve pain in the stomach by blocking the production of prostaglandins, which are chemical mediators that cause pain and inflammation.
Quinidine: It has antispasmodic properties, which help relax the muscles in the stomach and reduce spasms that can cause pain.
Cinchonine: It has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help reduce inflammation and relieve muscle spasms in the stomach, providing relief from pain and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the primary source of quinine, a natural compound that helps alleviate stomach pain and inflammation.
Barks: They contain a high concentration of quinine and other alkaloids, which are used to treat various types of stomach pain and discomfort.
Stems: They are also a source of quinine and other beneficial compounds that help reduce inflammation and alleviate stomach pain.
remedy_recipe: Collect 250g of dried cinchona officinalis bark and sift it to remove any lumps.
Weigh out 5g of the dried bark and add it to a mortar and pestle.
Grind the cinchona officinalis bark into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix 1g of the powdered cinchona officinalis with 1g of vegetable cellulose powder in a bowl.
Fill 10 empty capsules with the mixture and seal them immediately to preserve potency.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita capsules helps with stomach pain because they contain peppermint oil, a natural anti-inflammatory agent that soothes the digestive system. The menthol content in peppermint oil helps relax the muscles in the stomach, reducing spasms and cramps that cause pain. Additionally, peppermint oil's calming effects on the nervous system may help alleviate nausea and bloating, promoting a feeling of comfort and relief from stomach discomfort. This natural remedy provides quick and effective relief from stomach pain.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: Helps with stomach pain by reducing inflammation and relaxing the muscles in the digestive tract, which eases cramps and spasms.
Caryophyllene: Acts as an anti-inflammatory agent, reducing swelling and pain in the stomach, and also has a calming effect on the digestive system.
Linalool: Exhibits anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help to reduce inflammation and ease muscle spasms in the stomach, providing relief from pain.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their essential oils and compounds like menthol and menthone, which provide relief from stomach pain.
Leaves: The leaves are also used because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe the stomach.
Leaves: The essential oils extracted from the leaves are effective in relaxing the digestive system and reducing stomach pain.
remedy_recipe: Dry 500 grams of fresh plant material to 10% moisture content over 7 days.
Grind 200 grams of the dried material into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix 1 part of the powdered plant material with 1 part of a cellulose-based filler powder.
Fill 200 empty gelatin capsules with 500 milligrams of the mixture.
Store the filled capsules in a cool dry place for up to 2 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cassia angustifolia capsules helps with stomach pain because they contain senna glycosides, which have anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the stomach lining and reduce inflammation. The natural laxative properties of the capsules also help to prevent constipation, a common cause of stomach discomfort. Additionally, Cassia angustifolia capsules have been traditionally used to relieve symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and other gastrointestinal disorders, providing fast and effective relief from stomach pain and discomfort. Regular use can promote overall digestive health.
remedy_constituents: Sennosides: These anthraquinone glycosides help with stomach pain by stimulating bowel movements, reducing constipation, and relieving intestinal cramps.
Angustifolin: This flavonoid glycoside has anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, which help to soothe and calm the stomach lining, reducing pain and inflammation.
Quercetin: This flavonoid has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the stomach, alleviating pain and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Cassia angustifolia are used due to their sennosides content, which helps in treating stomach pain and constipation.
Seeds: The seeds of Cassia angustifolia are used to make capsules for stomach pain due to their laxative properties, which help in regulating bowel movements.
Roots: The roots of Cassia angustifolia are used for their medicinal properties, including treating stomach pain, due to their high sennosides content.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh cassia angustifolia roots in the early morning after 3-4 years of cultivation with sufficient sunlight.
Dry the harvested roots in a clean, well-ventilated area at 50-60°c for 24-48 hours to reduce moisture content.
Grind 100-200 grams of dried roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle with a ratio of 1:10.
Mix 5-10 grams of the powder with a natural excipient like gum arabic to create a uniform blend.
Fill 100-150 empty capsules with 0.2-0.5 grams of the herbal blend and seal them properly for storage.

remedies.remedy_desc: Piper nigrum capsules helps with stomach pain because they contain the active compound piperine, which has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. When taken in capsule form, piperine helps to reduce inflammation and soothe the digestive tract, alleviating discomfort and pain. Additionally, piperine has natural carminative properties, which aid in the release of trapped gas and alleviate bloating, further contributing to its stomach-soothing effects and promoting overall digestive health.
remedy_constituents: Piperine: It helps reduce inflammation and pain in the stomach by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.
Flavonoids: These compounds have antioxidant properties, which help protect the stomach lining from damage caused by free radicals and reduce inflammation.
Terpenoids: They exhibit anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help alleviate stomach pain and discomfort by reducing inflammation in the stomach lining.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Used for their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties to alleviate stomach pain.
Fruits: Used for their carminative properties to reduce gas and alleviate stomach discomfort.
Leaves: Used for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties to soothe and calm the stomach.
remedy_recipe: Grind 20 grams of dried piper nigrum berries into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar pestle.
Mix 1 part of the ground powder with 2 parts of a filler material such as gelatin or plant-based capsule filler.
Fill a capsule machine with the powder mixture and adjust the machine to 500 mg per capsule.
Seal the capsules using the capsule machine and store them in an airtight container at room temperature.
Label the herbal piper nigrum capsules with the name of the product date of manufacture and recommended dosage.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba capsules helps with stomach pain because they contain antioxidants that reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating discomfort and cramps. The flavonoids present in Ginkgo biloba also help improve blood flow to the stomach, promoting digestion and reducing spasms. Additionally, Ginkgo biloba's anti-inflammatory properties may help soothe irritated stomach lining, providing relief from bloating, nausea, and other stomach issues, allowing for a more comfortable and relaxed digestive system. This makes Ginkgo biloba capsules a popular natural remedy for stomach pain.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds help reduce inflammation and improve blood flow to the stomach, which can help alleviate pain and discomfort.
Terpenoids (including bilobalide): They have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help protect the stomach lining and reduce irritation, leading to relief from stomach pain.

Phenolic acids (including glycosides): These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and protect the stomach lining, alleviating stomach pain and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Ginkgo biloba leaves are commonly used to make capsules for stomach pain due to their ability to improve blood flow and reduce inflammation in the digestive system.
Seeds: Ginkgo biloba seeds are used to make capsules for stomach pain because of their potential to reduce nausea and alleviate digestive discomfort.
Barks: Ginkgo biloba barks are used to make capsules for stomach pain due to their possible anti-inflammatory and soothing effects on the digestive tract.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 500 grams of ginkgo biloba dried leaves to be used for capsule production.
Crush the ginkgo biloba leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 250 milliliters of distilled water with 2 grams of gum arabic in a bowl to create a liquid binder.
Combine 1 gram of the ginkgo biloba powder with 1 milliliter of the gum arabic mixture to create a uniform mixture.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 500 milligrams of the ginkgo biloba powder mixture using a capsule filler for 10 minutes.
